title: Degradation Tier Report v1
description: Runtime degradation status for an ecosystem tool. Describes whether a tool is available, which tier it belongs to, and which capabilities are affected if it becomes unavailable. Producer: any tool that monitors its own health or its dependencies' availability. Consumer: cortina, volva, canopy, agent runtimes, and operator dashboards.

tool.description: Name of the tool being reported. Must be lowercase alphanumeric with underscores. Examples: 'hyphae', 'volva', 'canopy', 'cortina'.

tier.description: Degradation tier: 1 (Critical, blocks agent loop), 2 (Optional, degrades quality), or 3 (Enhancement, reduces observability only).

available.description: Whether the tool is available and operating normally. If false, the agent must follow the behavior contract for the given tier.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why the tool became unavailable or started degrading. Examples: 'connection refused on unix socket', 'startup timeout after 30s', 'out of disk space'.

degraded_capabilities.description: List of capabilities or features that are unavailable or reduced when this tool is not available. Examples for hyphae: ['memory recall', 'memoir lookup', 'session timeline']. Empty array if available=true.

timestamp.description: Optional. ISO 8601 timestamp of when the degradation or availability change was detected. Helps correlate failures with logs.

suggestion.description: Optional. Guidance for operators or the agent on how to recover. Examples: 'restart the service', 'check connection settings', 'free up disk space'.
